vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.6.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-457: Conversation discovery — index sessions inside and outside Panopticon

plan.title: Conversation discovery — index sessions inside and outside Panopticon
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-6

narratives.Problem: Panopticon only sees conversations it spawned. Ad-hoc `claude` sessions started from a terminal — debugging, exploration, one-off fixes — are invisible. The user has hundreds of such JSONL sessions under ~/.claude/projects with no way to search, review, correlate cost, or learn from them.
narratives.Proposal: Add a `conversations` subsystem: a new discovered_sessions SQLite table indexed from ~/.claude/projects JSONLs via a scripted (zero-LLM) scanner with adaptive parallelism; three-tier search (structured filters + SQLite FTS5 + optional vector embeddings); three-level user-initiated enrichment (quick/deep/custom-model) reusing Panopticon's model-fallback router; a Mission Control Conversations panel with faceted search and enrichment controls. All five phases ship in this issue.

items.title: Add discovered_sessions, sessions_fts, session_embeddings tables + migration

narrative.Action: In src/lib/database/schema.ts, add idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for discovered_sessions (per spec columns), sessions_fts (FTS5 virtual table with BM25 + column weights), session_embeddings (BLOB storage). Add all indexes per spec. Add the same tables to the per-workspace DB block. Create src/lib/database/discovered-sessions-db.ts with CRUD, FTS sync, and embedding helpers. Include a vitest suite mirroring existing conversations-db tests.

subItems.title: All three tables created idempotently in main + per-workspace DB on startup
discovered-sessions-db.ts exposes insert/upsert, findByFilters, searchFTS, insertEmbedding, topKCosine, getByJsonlPath
Upsert is idempotent on (jsonl_path) — re-inserting same row updates file_size/file_mtime without duplicates
Unit tests cover CRUD, filter composition, FTS insert/search, and embedding storage round-trip

items.title: Async streaming JSONL parser for scanner use in dashboard server

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/conversations/jsonl-async.ts. Uses fs/promises + readline streaming to parse a JSONL and return { messageCount, firstTs, lastTs, modelsUsed, primaryModel, tokenInput, tokenOutput, toolsUsed[], filesTouched[], cwdFromFirstMessage }. Zero sync FS calls. Do NOT modify the existing sync cost-parsers/jsonl-parser.ts — the CLI cost command still uses it. Reuse its ClaudeMessage type.

subItems.title: Parses message_count, timestamps, models, token totals from a sample JSONL correctly (vitest fixture)
Extracts tools_used and files_touched arrays from tool_use blocks (Edit/Write/Read paths, Bash commands excluded)
Reads cwd from first JSONL message when present; returns null if absent or unparseable
Handles corrupt/empty/partial JSONLs without throwing — returns partial metadata
Uses only fs/promises + stream APIs — no sync FS calls (verified by lint rule or review)

items.title: Claude project hash → workspace path resolver

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/conversations/hash-resolver.ts. Primary strategy: use cwd from the first JSONL message (from async parser). Fallback strategy: build a reverse map by walking configured watchDirs + projects.yaml entries, encoding each with existing encodeClaudeProjectDir (src/lib/paths.ts), and keying by hash. On hash collision between multiple known workspaces, prefer an exact match if any JSONL row hints at it; otherwise return null and log a warning. Cache the reverse map per scan run.

subItems.title: JSONL-cwd primary path resolves correctly for a session with a cwd field
Reverse-map fallback resolves a hash to a known workspace when JSONL has no cwd
Returns null (not crashes) when hash is unknown and no JSONL cwd exists
Reverse map is built once per scan run (cached), not per session

items.title: SystemCapabilities probe for adaptive scan parallelism

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/conversations/system-probe.ts. Detects cpuCores (os.cpus().length), driveType (lsblk -d -o name,rota via execAsync on Linux; 'unknown' on macOS/Windows), driveReadMBps (read a 10MB sample file using fs/promises, measure elapsed), availableMemoryMB (os.freemem / 1024 / 1024). Cache result per-process. Parallelism strategy table from spec: SSD → min(cpuCores, 16); HDD → 2; unknown → 4. Respect optional config override conversations.scanMaxParallel.

subItems.title: Probe returns SystemCapabilities with all four fields populated on Linux (SSD case)
Gracefully falls back to 'unknown' driveType on macOS/Windows without throwing
Chosen parallelism matches spec table (SSD→min(cores,16), HDD→2, unknown→4)
conversations.scanMaxParallel config override takes precedence over probe result
Probe uses execAsync + fs/promises — zero sync FS or execSync calls

items.title: Scanner orchestrator with bounded parallelism work pool

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/conversations/scanner.ts and work-pool.ts. The scanner accepts { mode: 'targeted'|'watched'|'system', dirs?: string[], onProgress?: (p) => void } and emits progress events. Walks ~/.claude/projects/*/ for *.jsonl files (async readdir), filters by mode (targeted → under given dirs; watched → under config watchDirs; system → all). Uses a bounded promise work pool sized by system-probe. For each JSONL: stat for (size, mtime), skip if unchanged vs DB row, else parse async, resolve hash via hash-resolver, correlate against conversations + cost_events tables, insert/upsert into discovered_sessions. SQLite writes serialized through a batch queue. Build a correlator.ts helper that queries existing conversations.session_file and cost_events.session_id for Panopticon-managed detection.

subItems.title: Targeted mode scans only JSONLs whose resolved workspace is under the given dirs
Watched mode reads dirs from config.yaml conversations.watchDirs
System mode discovers all sessions under ~/.claude/projects
Change detection: re-scan of unchanged file does not re-parse JSONL (verified via parser call counter in test)
Work pool respects maxParallel from system-probe; concurrent reads never exceed limit
Progress callback fires with { dirsProcessed, dirsTotal, sessionsFound, elapsedMs }
Sessions matching existing conversations.session_file are marked panopticon_managed=1 with pan_issue_id + pan_agent_id populated
Permission-denied directories are skipped with a warning, not a crash
Cost estimate populated from token counts using model-capabilities.ts pricing

narrative.Action: Register `pan conversations` parent command and `scan` subcommand in src/cli/commands/conversations/. Wires through the scanner service. Renders live progress with a simple stderr bar (cliProgressOrMinimal) including the format from spec: [████] 847/1203 dirs | 2341 sessions found | 12.3s. --dry-run only reports what would be scanned (counts by dir).

subItems.title: `pan conversations scan ~/Projects/foo` scans and reports inserted/updated/skipped counts
`pan conversations scan --watched` reads watchDirs from config
`pan conversations scan --system` walks entire ~/.claude/projects
`--dry-run` performs no DB writes; prints planned counts
Progress bar renders with matching format to spec

items.title: Search composer: structured filters + FTS5 + semantic + output formats

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/conversations/search.ts. Parses filter flags (--workspace/--model/--managed/--unmanaged/--since/--before/--after/--min-cost/--max-cost/--min-messages/--tag/--tool/--file/--issue/--enriched/--not-enriched) into a typed ConversationFilter, composes SQL with AND logic, runs FTS5 MATCH when a free-text query is given (with BM25 ranking, column weighting summary_detailed=2/summary=1.5/tags=1/files=0.5), and intersects with semantic results when --semantic or --similar is set. Relative time parsing (7d, 24h, 30m) via existing duration util. --format table|json|brief|ids renders via a small formatter module.

subItems.title: All structured filters compose with AND and produce correct SQL
--since 7d, --before ISO date, --after ISO date all parse and filter correctly
--tag/--tool/--file match against JSON array columns
FTS5 query with prefix (refact*), phrase ("error handling"), boolean (redis AND cache NOT test) all return BM25-ranked results
Filters + FTS compose: FTS query runs within the filtered set
--format table|json|brief|ids all produce correct shape

items.title: CLI: search, list, show, cost subcommands

narrative.Action: In src/cli/commands/conversations/: add search.ts (wires to search composer), list.ts (--managed/--unmanaged, defaults to recent), show.ts (full detail for a session id including messages/cost/models/duration/tools/files/summaries), cost.ts (aggregate --by workspace|model).

subItems.title: `pan conversations search "query"` runs FTS and prints table results
`pan conversations list --managed` lists only panopticon_managed=1 rows
`pan conversations show <id>` prints full session detail including current enrichment level
`pan conversations cost --by workspace` matches sum of discovered_sessions.estimated_cost grouped by workspace_path

items.title: Tiered enrichment engine (L1 quick / L2 deep / L3 custom) via model-fallback router

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/conversations/enrichment/. index.ts is enrich(sessionIds, level, opts). level1.ts samples first + last + 1 middle message, calls Haiku tier via existing model-fallback.ts, produces { summary, tags[] }. level2.ts samples first 3 + last 3 + 5 middle + tool_use summaries, calls Sonnet tier, produces { summary_detailed, tags[] }. level3.ts takes a user-specified model id; sample by default or full transcript if --full; optional --prompt appended to system prompt. Writes update discovered_sessions, bump enrichment_level, record enrichment_model + enriched_at, and sync FTS5. Subagent parallelism = bounded promise pool (config.conversations.enrichment.maxParallel, default 4). Cost estimation before each batch; prompts confirmation when total estimated cost exceeds costConfirmThreshold. Auto-embed on L2+ if conversations.embeddingAutoOnDeep.

subItems.title: Level 1 sends exactly 3 messages (first + last + 1 middle) to Haiku tier
Level 2 sends 11 messages + tool_use summaries to Sonnet tier
Level 3 with --with <model> calls the specified model; --full sends the entire transcript
--prompt "..." is appended to the enrichment system prompt
Re-enrichment preserves level-1 summary while overwriting summary_detailed and tags
Cost estimate printed and user prompted [y/N] when total exceeds costConfirmThreshold
Model API failure marks session as enrichment_failed (does not crash pool)
FTS5 rows are upserted after each successful enrichment
Enrichment goes through src/lib/model-fallback.ts (respects provider disabled state)
Cost estimate vs actual from cost_events stays within 20% on sample run

items.title: CLI: pan conversations enrich with all flags

narrative.Action: Add src/cli/commands/conversations/enrich.ts. Flags per spec: --limit, --workspace, --since, --deep, --with <model>, --full, --prompt, --upgrade. Selects target session set, calls enrichment engine, renders per-session progress + final totals (sessions processed, cost actual, failures).

subItems.title: `pan conversations enrich --limit 50` enriches up to 50 un-enriched sessions at level 1
`--deep --upgrade` converts all level-1 sessions to level 2
`--with claude-opus-4-6 --full <id>` runs level 3 with full transcript
All flag combinations from spec CLI section parse and execute

items.title: Embedding providers + pure-JS cosine similarity + semantic search

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/conversations/embeddings/. providers/openai.ts, voyage.ts, ollama.ts all implement EmbeddingProvider { embed(texts: string[]): Promise<Float32Array[]> }. cosine.ts implements pure-JS cosine over Float32Array in memory (normalize once, dot product). index.ts is embed(sessionIds). Selection via config.conversations.embeddingProvider. Stored as BLOB in session_embeddings with dim + model. Search integration: search --semantic "text" embeds the query with the same provider, loads all embeddings into memory, computes topK by cosine, optionally intersects with structured filters. --similar <id> loads that session's embedding and does the same comparison.

subItems.title: OpenAI provider calls text-embedding-3-small and stores 1536-dim Float32 BLOB
Voyage provider calls voyage-code-3 and stores 1024-dim BLOB
Ollama provider calls nomic-embed-text via local HTTP and stores BLOB
Cosine function ranks identical vectors as 1.0 and orthogonal as 0.0 (unit test)
`pan conversations embed` embeds all enriched un-embedded sessions
`pan conversations embed --regenerate` overwrites all embeddings
`pan conversations embed --status` prints indexed/embedded counts + model name
`pan conversations search --semantic "race condition"` returns cosine-ranked results
`pan conversations search --similar <id>` returns sessions ranked by similarity to that id
Missing API key produces an actionable error (not a silent crash)

items.title: RPC + events contracts for conversation subsystem

narrative.Action: In packages/contracts/src/: add DiscoveredSession + ConversationFilter + search result types to types.ts; add conversation RPC procs (scan, search, listDiscovered, showDiscovered, enrich, embed, cost) to rpc.ts; add domain events scan.started/scan.progress/scan.complete/enrich.progress/enrich.complete/embed.progress to events.ts. Rebuild contracts package.

subItems.title: All new types exported from @panopticon/contracts and typecheck passes project-wide
New RPC procs appear in PanRpcGroup with Effect Schema
Domain events are added to event-reducers.ts so the frontend store receives them

items.title: Dashboard server routes + service for conversations

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts (Effect HTTP routes: POST /scan, GET /search, GET /list, GET /:id, POST /enrich, POST /embed, GET /cost) and src/dashboard/server/services/conversation-index.ts (service layer wrapping src/lib/conversations, emitting domain events during scan/enrich/embed). All FS access must be async (fs/promises + execAsync per CLAUDE.md). Register routes in server.ts.

subItems.title: All seven endpoints return correct shapes and pass Effect Schema validation
Scan progress emits domain events on /ws/rpc subscribeDomainEvents stream
Enrichment progress emits per-session domain events with model + cost
Zero execSync/readFileSync/writeFileSync/readdirSync/statSync in routes or service (grep + review)

items.title: Dashboard Conversations panel: search, facets, detail, enrichment controls

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/conversations/. ConversationsPanel.tsx is the top-level panel added to Mission Control navigation. ConversationSearchBar.tsx combines a free-text input (FTS), filter chips (workspace/model/managed/tag/tool/file/since), and a semantic toggle when embeddings enabled. ConversationTimeline.tsx renders grouped, faceted results (workspace/model/time/cost/enrichment-level) with click-to-filter. ConversationDetail.tsx is a drawer with messages, cost, models, duration, tools, files, summaries at each level, and EnrichmentControls.tsx buttons (quick/deep/custom-model). FacetPanel.tsx shows counts. All data flows through RPC client (WsTransport) and the Zustand store receives scan/enrich progress events.

subItems.title: Conversations panel appears in Mission Control nav and loads recent sessions on open
Search bar: free-text, filter chips, semantic toggle all work and compose
Faceted results group by workspace/model/time/cost/enrichment-level; clicking a facet filters
Detail drawer shows full session with visual distinction between managed and ad-hoc sessions
Enrichment buttons trigger RPC calls and show live progress via domain events
Aggregate cost view matches `pan conversations cost --by workspace` output
Manual browser test: scan + search + enrich + detail view all work end-to-end

items.title: config.yaml schema additions for conversations subsystem

narrative.Action: Extend src/lib/config.ts / config-yaml.ts with the conversations block: watchDirs (default ['~/Projects']), scanMaxParallel (null = auto), embeddings (false), embeddingProvider ('openai'), embeddingModel ('text-embedding-3-small'), embeddingAutoOnDeep (true), enrichment.quickModel (null), enrichment.deepModel (null), enrichment.maxParallel (4), enrichment.costConfirmThreshold (1.00). Add migration for existing config files.

subItems.title: New conversations config block loads with defaults when missing from config.yaml
Config migration leaves existing user values intact and fills defaults
Typed access via getConfig() for all new fields

items.title: End-to-end integration tests: scan → enrich → embed → search

narrative.Action: Add a vitest integration suite that (1) seeds a temp ~/.claude/projects fixture with 3 sample JSONLs, (2) runs the scanner against it, (3) runs L1 enrichment with a mocked model router, (4) generates embeddings with a stub provider, (5) runs structured + FTS + semantic searches and asserts results. Also assert cost aggregation and that re-scan on unchanged files skips parsing. Mirrors the test matrix from the spec.

subItems.title: Full pipeline test: scan → enrich → embed → search returns expected top result
Re-scan on unchanged files does not re-parse (parser mock call count unchanged)
Cost aggregation equals sum of individual session estimated_cost
FTS stays in sync after re-enrichment (old summary text no longer matches; new text matches)
Managed session is correctly linked to an issue_id when conversations table has a matching session_file
